senators.name: Mike Lee
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Brigham Young University and J.D. from BYU Law School (first in class). Clerked for Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito and Judge Dee Benson on the U.S. District Court. Assistant U.S. Attorney. Constitutional law expertise demonstrated through Supreme Court clerkship and extensive constitutional scholarship. Deep analytical approach to legal and constitutional questions.
education: J.D., Brigham Young University Law School
experience: Law Clerk to Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito

senators.name: John Curtis
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Brigham Young University and M.B.A. from BYU Marriott School of Business. Career as tech executive and mayor of Provo. Business and management background. Known for climate and clean energy engagement within GOP but overall approach is more pragmatic than analytically rigorous.

UT-01.name: Blake Moore
UT-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Utah State University and M.B.A. from Northwestern University (Kellogg). Career as business executive. Northwestern Kellogg MBA is elite analytical training. However, legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.

UT-02.name: Celeste Maloy
UT-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Brigham Young University and J.D. from BYU Law School. Career as attorney specializing in natural resources and public lands. Legal training provides analytical foundation but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Brigham Young University Law School
experience: Counsel to Sen. Mike Lee and Rep. Chris Stewart

UT-03.name: Mike Kennedy
UT-03.nerdReason: B.S. from Brigham Young University, M.D. from University of Pennsylvania School of Medicine, and J.D. from BYU Law School. One of very few members of Congress holding both an M.D. and J.D. UPenn medical degree from an Ivy League institution. Practiced as a family physician. Combines deep medical scientific expertise with legal analytical training — an exceptionally rare dual credential.
education: J.D., Brigham Young University Law School
M.D., University of Pennsylvania School of Medicine

UT-04.name: Burgess Owens
UT-04.notNerdReason: B.S. from University of Miami. Career as NFL safety (Oakland Raiders, New York Jets) and later businessman and author. Athletic and business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
experience: NFL Player (Oakland Raiders Super Bowl champion, New York Jets)
